findings.title: Retire stale hard-completion monitoring request
evidence: AI_OVERSIGHT_REQUEST.md still marks the Sunday 12 July 2026 hard-completion directive as active monitoring and at_risk.
Latest published close-out metadata records Sprint 1615 closed, milestone 1630-1639 complete, no autonomous next sprint, and only write_audit_entry as a standing deferred owner action.
findings.whyItMatters: The expired active request can cause workers or oversight automation to reopen completed roadmap work, continue reporting an obsolete deadline, or mistake deliberate owner-only deferral for autonomous delivery failure.
findings.recommendedAction: Archive or close the 2026-07-06 request, replace its status with roadmap complete, and retain only the write_audit_entry 0/3 standing deferred owner gate plus production-host verification as current owner-controlled follow-up. Do not queue Sprint 1616 or a new autonomous milestone.

findings.title: Drawing supersession does not invalidate governed knowledge
evidence: The current public state says template review and publication payloads carry templateRevisionId, while linked drawing revision IDs are optional viewer context and are not persisted in Memory audit payloads.
drawing_revision_superseded events exist, but there is no knowledge invalidation or revalidation hook, and the knowledge pipeline does not consume drawing-revision evidence links.
findings.whyItMatters: A lesson or governed knowledge item derived from a superseded drawing can remain approved and be reused by Project B without traceable source-revision lineage. This breaks the Project A to governed knowledge to Project B trust loop and creates construction, commercial, and audit risk.
findings.recommendedAction: Add immutable sourceRevisionIds and sourceProjectId lineage to lesson capture and knowledge publication records. On drawing_revision_superseded, mark dependent knowledge stale or revalidation_required, block cross-project recommendation until re-approved, and add an end-to-end test proving Project A revision supersession prevents stale learning from reaching Project B while preserving the full audit chain.

findings.title: Oversight inbound consumption and canonical state publication are stalled
evidence: AI_OVERSIGHT_INBOX.json still contains the 18 July and 19 July findings with status new and no published triage or acknowledgement.
AI_OVERSIGHT_PROTOCOL.md requires inbound pull and consume-oversight-findings.ts at sprint close-out, with dispositions recorded in canonical state.
Authenticated AI_OVERSIGHT_STATE.json is empty, while the public raw state is malformed historical content; authenticated and public request and packet views also remain materially inconsistent.

The latest authenticated packet is dated 12 July and contains contradictory next-sprint metadata, so current Validation 3/3, Migration 015 post-sync verification, RLS 7/7, production probe status, and Project A to Project B learning governance cannot be reliably verified.
findings.whyItMatters: The oversight channel is no longer closing its own control loop. High data-integrity findings can remain untriaged, workers can act on different repository views, and production readiness claims cannot be tied to one canonical, machine-readable state.
findings.recommendedAction: Run the established oversight pull and consumer now, triage every new finding, regenerate AI_OVERSIGHT_STATE.json as valid JSON from the delivery repository, reconcile request and packet metadata, push one sanitized canonical snapshot, and verify identical blob hashes across authenticated contents API, raw public main, and the delivery-side sync checkout before the next close-out. Do not reconstruct state from the public malformed file.

findings.title: Production host renders a blank page after Production Ready sign-off
evidence: AI_OVERSIGHT_REQUEST.md, AI_OVERSIGHT_RESPONSE.md, and OVERSIGHT_PACKET.md all declare Production Ready and cite release 20260722T201518Z-prod-gate3 with live-host acceptance passed.

A current browser check of https://ccc.247print.biz shows the document returning HTTP 200 while the user sees a blank application page, indicating the host is reachable but the SPA is not rendering successfully.
The current machine-readable AI_OVERSIGHT_STATE.json remains empty, so no canonical post-release regression state or production probe result is available.
findings.whyItMatters: A blank production SPA is a release-blocking regression that contradicts the Production Ready claim. Users cannot access the system, and the existing acceptance evidence is no longer sufficient to represent current production health.
findings.recommendedAction: Pause only work that could overwrite the production deployment, diagnose the live browser console and network waterfall, verify index.html asset references, /assets responses, production environment variables, CSP, SPA document root, and atomic release symlink, then redeploy or roll back to the last known-good release. Re-run full acceptance against https://ccc.247print.biz in a clean browser and update oversight state with timestamped console, network, release hash, and production probe evidence before restoring Production Ready status.
